section: Chapter 13: Our Home: Earth, a Unique Life Sustaining Planet

questions.question: Why is Earth considered a unique planet in the solar system?
questions.explanation: Earth has water, atmosphere, and suitable temperature conditions that allow living organisms to survive.

questions.question: What region around a star allows liquid water to exist?
questions.explanation: The habitable or Goldilocks zone has temperatures suitable for liquid water to exist.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Blue Planet?
questions.explanation: Earth appears blue from space because most of its surface is covered by water.

questions.question: Why is Venus the hottest planet in the solar system?
questions.correct_answer: Because of its thick carbon dioxide atmosphere
questions.explanation: Venus experiences an extreme greenhouse effect due to its dense atmosphere trapping heat.

questions.question: Which atmospheric layer protects Earth from harmful ultraviolet rays?
questions.explanation: The ozone layer absorbs most of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet radiation.

questions.question: What protects Earth from harmful cosmic rays and solar wind?
questions.explanation: Earth's magnetic field deflects charged particles coming from space.

questions.question: What process do plants use to produce food using sunlight?
questions.explanation: Plants convert carbon dioxide and water into food using sunlight and release oxygen.

questions.question: What are the four major Earth systems that interact to sustain life?
questions.explanation: These systems interact continuously to maintain Earth's environmental balance.

questions.question: What ensures the continuity of life on Earth?
questions.explanation: Reproduction allows organisms to produce offspring and maintain their species.

questions.question: What are the three major environmental challenges facing Earth today?
questions.correct_answer: Climate change, biodiversity loss, and pollution
questions.explanation: These interconnected problems are known as the triple planetary crisis.

notes.title: Habitable Zone
notes.content: The habitable zone is the region around a star where temperatures allow water to remain in liquid form.

notes.title: Earth’s Atmosphere
notes.content: The atmosphere is a layer of gases surrounding Earth that supports life and protects it from harmful radiation.

notes.title: Magnetic Field
notes.content: Earth’s magnetic field acts as a protective shield against cosmic rays and solar wind.

notes.title: Earth Systems
notes.content: The atmosphere, hydrosphere, geosphere, and biosphere interact to sustain life on Earth.

notes.title: Reproduction
notes.content: Reproduction allows living organisms to pass genetic information to offspring and maintain species continuity.

facts.fact: About 70% of Earth's surface is covered with water.
The Earth's crust where life exists is extremely thin compared to the size of the planet.
Earth behaves like a giant magnet due to movement of molten iron in its core.
Even small changes in temperature or atmospheric composition can affect life on Earth.
